title: symphytum hajastanum
intro: Symphytum hajastanum, also known as Armenian comfrey, is a perennial herb belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to the Caucasus region. The plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and wound-healing properties. The health benefits of Symphytum hajastanum include reducing inflammation and pain, promoting wound healing, and supporting bone and tissue repair. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, sprains, and skin irritations.
